notes.note: The Nones fell on the 7th of March, May, July and October and the 5th of the other months; here the reference is to a date Varro had proposed for some meeting or undertaking in mid-46 BC. Cicero approves it both on public grounds and because of the season.

Cicero alludes to his decision to join Pompey and the senatorial cause in the civil war of 49-48 BC. He defends it as a choice of duty over hope, and contrasts himself favourably with those who never left Italy and with the irreconcilables who refused to return after Pharsalus.

Cicero criticizes the unbending censoriousness of those former Pompeians who, having stayed safely aloof, now reproach men like himself and Varro for accepting Caesar's pardon and resuming a quiet life under the new regime.

Cicero's villa near Cumae on the Bay of Naples, one of his several country houses; the Tusculan villa near Tusculum (south-east of Rome) was another. The casual mention of having the bath made ready reflects the easy intimacy of the Varro correspondence.
